mnemonics.title: Binary States
mnemonics.content: DOGS BARK LOUD: Digital Output Gets Binary Logic States - 0 is OFF/LOW, 1 is ON/HIGH

mnemonics.title: Digital Advantages
mnemonics.content: NOISE PIG: Noise immunity, Operation reliability, Infinite reproduction, Storage easy, Error correction, Programmability, Integration, Greater precision

acronyms.usage: A type of digital circuit technology that uses transistors for logic operations
Another digital circuit technology known for low power consumption
The process of creating integrated circuits by combining thousands of transistors
Languages used to describe digital hardware for simulation and synthesis

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a light switch - it is either OFF (0) or ON (1). Multiple switches together can represent larger numbers just like multiple bits represent larger values.
Think of a staircase (digital) versus a ramp (analog). The staircase has discrete steps while the ramp varies smoothly.
Think of logic gates as decision makers - AND needs both inputs to be true, OR needs at least one, NOT simply inverts the input.
Picture a thermometer with two colored zones - blue zone (0-0.8V) represents 0, green zone (2-5V) represents 1, gray zone (0.8-2V) is forbidden

analogies.analogy: Like a light switch that is either completely ON or completely OFF - there is no in-between state, unlike a dimmer switch which is analog.
Like counting people - you cannot have half a person. The count changes in whole number steps, not continuously.
Like a door that opens only when you have both a key AND a code (AND gate), or a door that opens when you have either a key OR a code (OR gate).
Like LEGO building - you have simple building blocks (logic gates) that can be combined in countless ways to create complex structures (digital systems).
